body.p:
Following the effects of the Body of
Christ. Just as it was said by Reymundus:
"This sacrament
of the Body of Chrit accomplishes
enetrnity and immortality
for those who take it worthy, and the opposite [for those who don't]"
The Apostle [says]:
"Let a man examine himself
and thus is understood what is said. Carefully
consider what is served to you
certainly such as the host of Christ." Hence
the verse: White, wheaten, thin
not large, round,
without yeast, not
false, the host of Christ should be. Gloss:
White: signifies chastity,
thus you should be white
that is chaste in body and heart.
Wheaten: wheat is the sweetest
of all grain, so you should
be sweet and gentle.
Thin: that is moderate in food
and drink. Not large: that is
humble, not proud. Round: such as
a round shape is moveable so
you should be moveable for the divine
office and for [doing certain] good
deeds. Without yeast:
yeast signifies envy and
anger, that is [you] should
have a [sincere heart] for God and the close ones
of yours. Not false: that is without
greed and avarice. Hence
Jerome [says]: I do not remember having read
of anyone being damned who
in this world led a life
[that was] decent. That is [to have a] great will
to work and so on. Following
as Ecclesiasticus says: [Who] worthily
takes the sacrament
receives thirteen blessings.
Hence the verse: I increase, I mingle, I sooth
I restrain, I terrify, I cleanse
I ignite, I enlighten, I double
I strengthen, I draw near, I lead, and
I give life. Upon the table so many dishes
I place. Gloss: I increase:
namely grace in the present and glory
in the future. I mingle: that is I [make one] gentler
I sooth: namely temptations.
I reastrain: that is evil
affection and the desire of the flesh.
I terrify: namely demons, for nothing
do the demons fear more
than the Body of Christ. Hence
Chrystostomus says: The Body of Christ repels
demons and calls angels
and rightly so, because where [their] Lord
is, his agnels are [too].
I cleanse: namely venial sins.
I ignite: namely [your] affection and spirit
toward good. I enlighten:
namely your understating. I double:
that is I multiply merit here
and merit there. I strengthen: namely
the virtues [within] you. I draw near: that is
I shall be familiar in [your] soul.
I lead: namely out of misery
and mourning to joy.
I give: namely eternal life.
A M E N
